compounds.brief: Enumeration describing change of text object.

compounds.name: Dali::Accessibility::ObjectPropertyChangeEvent
compounds.brief: Enumeration describing change of object property.

compounds.name: Dali::Accessibility::WindowEvent
compounds.brief: Enumeration describing change of window object.

compounds.name: Dali::Accessibility::WindowRestoreType
compounds.brief: Restored window state.

compounds.name: Dali::Accessibility::TextBoundary
compounds.brief: Enumeration used to acquire bounded text from accessible object having textual content.

members.name: WORD
members.brief: Only one character is acquired.

members.name: SENTENCE
members.brief: Not supported.

members.name: LINE
members.brief: Not supported.

members.name: PARAGRAPH
members.brief: Not supported.

members.name: MAX_COUNT
members.brief: Not supported.

compounds.name: Dali::Accessibility::Gesture
compounds.brief: Enumeration describing type of gesture.

compounds.name: Dali::Accessibility::GestureState
compounds.brief: Enumeration indicating current state of gesture.

compounds.name: Dali::Accessibility::ReadingInfoType
compounds.brief: Enumeration of ReadingInfoType.

compounds.name: Dali::Accessibility::AtspiInterface
compounds.brief: Enumeration of all AT-SPI interfaces.

compounds.name: Dali::Accessibility::AtspiEvent
compounds.brief: Enumeration of all AT-SPI events.

members.name: Say
members.brief: Reads given text by screen reader.
members.signature: DALI_ADAPTOR_API void Say(const std::string &text, bool discardable, std::function< void(std::string)> callback)

params.name: discardable
params.description: If TRUE, reading can be discarded by subsequent reading requests, if FALSE the reading must finish before next reading request can be started

params.name: callback
params.description: the callback function that is called on reading signals emitted during processing of this reading request. Callback can be one of the following signals: ReadingCancelled, ReadingStopped, ReadingSkipped

members.name: Pause
members.brief: Force accessibility client to pause.

members.name: Resume
members.brief: Force accessibility client to resume.

members.name: StopReading
members.brief: Cancels anything screen-reader is reading / has queued to read.

params.name: alsoNonDiscardable
params.description: whether to cancel non-discardable readings as well

members.name: SuppressScreenReader
members.brief: Suppresses reading of screen-reader.

params.name: suppress
params.description: whether to suppress reading of screen-reader

members.name: IsEnabled
members.brief: Returns whether the state of Accessibility is enabled or not.
members.returns: True if Accessibility is enabled, false otherwise.

members.name: IsScreenReaderEnabled
members.brief: Returns whether the state of Screen Reader is enabled or not.
members.returns: True if Screen Reader is enabled, false otherwise.

members.name: CustomCommandReceivedSignalType
members.brief: Signal signature for CustomCommandReceivedSignal.

members.name: AddIdleWithReturnValue
members.brief: Ensures that the function passed in is called from the main loop when it is idle.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type should be used: boolMyFunction(); This callback will be called repeatedly as long as it returns true. A return of 0 deletes this callback.
notes: Function must be called from main event thread only
Ownership of the callback is passed onto this class.
members.returns: true if added successfully, false otherwise

members.name: GetDataPath
members.brief: Gets the absolute path to the application's data directory which is used to store private data of the application.
members.returns: The absolute path to the application's data directory

members.name: DownCast
members.brief: Downcasts a ref object to Application handle.
members.detailed: If handle points to an Application object, the downcast produces valid base handle If not, the returned base handle is left uninitialized
members.returns: handle to an Application object or an uninitialized base handle

members.name: CustomCommandReceivedSignal
members.brief: This signal will be triggered when a custom command is received.
members.detailed: For this signal to be triggered, the adaptor must be built with -DENABLE_NETWORK_LOGGING=ON and when running, DALI_NETWORK_CONTROL=1 must also be set. A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(conststd::string&);
members.returns: The signal when a custom command is received

members.name: GetRenderThreadId
members.brief: Gets the render thread ID of DALi.
notes: Returns a valid thread ID only if the render thread has started and the system supports thread ID retrieval. Otherwise, returns 0.

members.name: GetUiThreadId
members.brief: Gets the UI thread ID of DALi.
notes: Returns a valid thread ID only if the adaptor has been initialized and the system supports thread ID retrieval. Otherwise, returns 0.

members.name: GetMainThreadId
members.brief: Gets the main thread ID of DALi.
notes: Returns a valid thread ID only if the system supports thread ID retrieval. Otherwise, returns 0.

members.name: FlushUpdateMessages
members.brief: Relayout the application and ensure all pending operations are flushed to the update thread.

members.name: SetApplicationLocale
members.brief: Set the application's language independently of the system language.

params.name: locale
params.description: Specifies the application's language and region in BCP 47 format (e.g., "en_US", "ko_KR").

members.name: GetPreInitializedWindow
members.brief: Get window from pre-initialized application.
notes: Only available pre-initialized application case. If application already created, it will return empty handle.
members.returns: Pre initialized window, or empty handle if no pre-initialized application exist.

members.name: GetCapturedBuffer
members.brief: Get PixelBuffer of captured image.
notes: GetCapturedBuffer is only available inside FinishedSignal, otherwise this returns empty handle.

members.name: GetDaliKeyCode
members.brief: Get the key code from a key name.
members.returns: The key code. -1 if the daliKey does not exist in the supported key lookup table.

members.name: DALI_KEY_DELETE
members.brief: Delete key.

members.name: DALI_KEY_CONTROL_LEFT
members.brief: Control Left key.

members.name: DALI_KEY_CONTROL_RIGHT
members.brief: Control Right key.

members.name: DALI_KEY_RETURN
members.brief: Return key.

members.name: DALI_KEY_KP_ENTER
members.brief: Keypad Enter key.

members.name: EncodeToFile
members.brief: Converts the current pixel contents to either a JPEG or PNG format and write that to the filesystem.
members.signature: bool EncodeToFile(NativeImage &image, const std::string &filename, const uint32_t quality)
members.returns: true if the pixels were written, and false otherwise

params.name: filename
params.description: Identify the filesystem location at which to write the encoded image. The extension determines the encoding used. The two valid encoding are (".jpeg"|".jpg") and ".png".

params.name: quality
params.description: The value to control image quality for jpeg file format in the range [1, 100]

members.name: AcquireBuffer
members.brief: Acquire buffer and information of an internal native image.
members.detailed: AcquireBuffer() and ReleaseBuffer() are a pair. ReleaseBuffer() MUST be called after AcquireBuffer().
notes: This locks a mutex until ReleaseBuffer is called.
members.returns: The buffer of an internal native image

members.name: ReleaseBuffer
members.brief: Release information of an internal native image.
members.detailed: AcquireBuffer() and ReleaseBuffer() are a pair. ReleaseBuffer() MUST be called after AcquireBuffer().
notes: The empty updatedArea means that the entire area has been changed.
This unlocks the mutex locked by AcquireBuffer.
members.returns: true If the buffer is released successfully, and false otherwise

params.name: updatedArea
params.description: The updated area of the buffer.

members.name: SetPixels
members.brief: Sets PixelBuffers to NativeImage.
notes: The width and height of the input pixel buffer should be same with those of NativeImage.
Only Pixel::Format::RGB888 and Pixel::Format::RGBA8888 are available as a input pixel format.
members.returns: true If the buffer is successfully set.

params.name: pixelFormat
params.description: Pixel format of the pixel buffer

members.name: SetResourceDestructionCallback
members.brief: Set the Resource Destruction Callback object.

params.name: ownedCallback
params.description: True if ownership of the callback is passed onto this class. False if ownership of the callback is exclude.

members.name: EnableBackBuffer
members.brief: Enable a back buffer.
notes: The front buffer will be copied to the back buffer before rendering if the front buffer is updated by AcquireBuffer and ReleaseBuffer.

params.name: enable
params.description: Whether a back buffer is enabled.

members.name: EventProcessingFinishedSignalType
members.brief: Event Processing finished signal type.

members.name: KeyEventSignalType
members.brief: Key event signal type.

members.name: TouchEventSignalType
members.brief: Touch signal type.

members.name: WheelEventSignalType
members.brief: Wheel signal type.

members.name: VisibilityChangedSignalType
members.brief: Visibility changed signal type.

members.name: TransitionEffectEventSignalType
members.brief: Effect signal type and state.

members.name: KeyboardRepeatSettingsChangedSignalType
members.brief: Keyboard repeat settings changed signal type.

members.name: AuxiliaryMessageSignalType
members.brief: Auxiliary message signal type.
members.aliased_type: Signal< void(const std::string &, const std::string &, const Property::Array &)>

members.name: AccessibilityHighlightSignalType
members.brief: Accessibility Highlight signal type.

members.name: InterceptKeyEventSignalType
members.brief: Intercept Key event signal type.

members.name: MovedSignalType
members.brief: Window Moved signal type.

members.name: OrientationChangedSignalType
members.brief: Window orientation changed signal type.

members.name: MouseInOutEventSignalType
members.brief: MouseInOutEvent signal type.

members.name: MouseRelativeEventSignalType
members.brief: MouseRelativeEvent signal type.

members.name: MoveCompletedSignalType
members.brief: Window Moved by Server signal type.

members.name: ResizeCompletedSignalType
members.brief: Window Resized by Server signal type.

members.name: InsetsChangedSignalType
members.brief: InsetsChanged signal type.

members.name: PointerConstraintsSignalType
members.brief: PointerConstraintsEvent signal type.

members.name: SetPositionSize
members.brief: Sets position and size of the window. This API guarantees that both moving and resizing of window will appear on the screen at once.

params.name: positionSize
params.description: The new window position and size

members.name: Get
members.brief: Retrieve the window that the given actor is added to.
members.returns: The window the actor is added to or an empty handle if the actor is not added to any window.

members.name: EventProcessingFinishedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted just after the event processing is finished.

members.name: WheelEventSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when wheel event is received.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(constWheelEvent&event);

members.name: VisibilityChangedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when the window is shown or hidden.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,boolvisible);

members.name: TransitionEffectEventSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted for transition effect.
members.detailed: The transition animation is appeared when the window is shown/hidden. When the animation is started, START signal is emitted. Then the animation is ended, END signal is emitted, too. A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,EffectStatestate,EffectTypetype);

members.name: KeyboardRepeatSettingsChangedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted just after the keyboard repeat setting is changed globally.

members.name: AuxiliaryMessageSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when window's auxiliary was changed then display server sent the message.
members.detailed: Auxiliary message is sent by display server. When client application added the window's auxiliary hint and if the auxiliary is changed, display server send the auxiliary message. Auxiliary message has the key, value and options.

members.name: AccessibilityHighlightSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when the window needs to grab or clear accessibility highlight. The highlight indicates that it is an object to interact with the user regardless of focus. After setting the highlight on the object, you can do things that the object can do, such as giving or losing focus.
members.detailed: This signal is emitted by Dali::Accessibility::Component::GrabHighlight and Dali::Accessibility::Component::ClearHighlight A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,boolhighlight);

members.name: MovedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when the window is moved.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,Dali::Window::WindowPositionposition); The parameters are the moved x and y coordinates. and window means this signal was called from what window

members.name: OrientationChangedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when the window orientation is changed.
members.detailed: To emit Window Orientation signal, AddAvailableOrientation() or SetPreferredOrientation() should be called before device is rotated. Most of cases, AddAvailableOrientation() or SetPreferredOrientation() is callled in onCreate(). A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,Dali::WindowOrientationorientation); The parameter is the changed window orientation. and window means this signal was called from what window

members.name: MoveCompletedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when window has been moved by the display server. To make the window move by display server, RequestMoveToServer() should be called. After the moving job is completed, this function will be called.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,Dali::Window::WindowPositionposition); The parameters are the moved x and y coordinates. and window means this signal was called from what window

members.name: ResizeCompletedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when window has been resized by the display server. To make the window move by display server, RequestResizeToServer() should be called. After the resizing job is completed, this function will be called.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,Dali::Window::WindowPositionposition); The parameters are the resized width and height coordinates. and window means this signal was called from what window

members.name: SetParent
members.brief: Sets parent window of the window.
members.detailed: After setting that, these windows do together when raise-up, lower and iconified/deiconified. Initially, the window is located on top of the parent. The window can go below parent by calling Lower(). If parent's window stack is changed by calling Raise() or Lower(), child windows are located on top of the parent again.

members.name: SetParent
members.brief: Sets parent window of the window.
members.detailed: After setting that, these windows do together when raise-up, lower and iconified/deiconified. This function has the additional flag whether the child is located above or below of the parent.
members.signature: void SetParent(Window window, Window parent, bool belowParent)

params.name: belowParent
params.description: The flag is whether the child is located above or below of the parent.

members.name: Unparent
members.brief: Unsets parent window of the window.
members.detailed: After unsetting, the window is disconnected his parent window.

members.name: GetParent
members.brief: Gets parent window of the window.
members.returns: The parent window of the window

members.name: GetCurrentOrientation
members.brief: Gets current orientation of the window.
members.returns: The current window orientation if previously set, or none

members.name: GetPhysicalOrientation
members.brief: Gets current physical orientation of the window.
members.detailed: It means current physical rotation angle of the window. If the height of the display device's area is greater than the width, default current orientation is PORTRAIT and current physical orientation angle is 0. If the width of the display device's area is greater than the height, default current orientation is LANDSCAPE and current physical orientation angle is 0.
members.returns: The current physical orientation degree of the window. It is one of them as 0, 90, 180 and 270.

members.name: SetAvailableOrientations
members.brief: Sets available orientations of the window.
members.detailed: This API is for setting several orientations one time.
members.signature: void SetAvailableOrientations(Window window, const Dali::Vector< WindowOrientation > &orientations)

params.name: orientations
params.description: The available orientation list to add

members.name: GetNativeId
members.brief: Gets the native window id.

members.name: AddFrameRenderedCallback
members.brief: Adds a callback that is called when the frame rendering is done by the graphics driver.
notes: A callback of the following type may be used: voidMyFunction(intframeId); This callback will be deleted once it is called.
Ownership of the callback is passed onto this class.
members.signature: void AddFrameRenderedCallback(Window window, CallbackBase *callback, int32_t frameId)

params.name: frameId
params.description: The Id to specify the frame. It will be passed when the callback is called.

members.name: AddFramePresentedCallback
members.brief: Adds a callback that is called when the frame is displayed on the display.
notes: A callback of the following type may be used: voidMyFunction(intframeId); This callback will be deleted once it is called.
Ownership of the callback is passed onto this class.
members.signature: void AddFramePresentedCallback(Window window, CallbackBase *callback, int32_t frameId)

params.name: frameId
params.description: The Id to specify the frame. It will be passed when the callback is called.

members.name: SetPositionSizeWithOrientation
members.brief: Sets window position and size for specific orientation. This api reserves the position and size per orientation to display server. When the device is rotated, the window is moved/resized with the reserved position/size by display server.
notes: Currently, it only works when the window's type is WindowType::IME.
To set WindowType::IME, use Application New(... WindowType type), not Window::SetType().
This function is only useful in Tizen world.
members.signature: void SetPositionSizeWithOrientation(Window window, PositionSize positionSize, WindowOrientation orientation)

params.name: positionSize
params.description: The reserved position and size for the orientation

members.name: RequestMoveToServer
members.brief: Requests to display server for the window is moved by display server.
members.detailed: This function should be called in mouse down event callback function. After this function is called in mouse down event callback function, the window is moved with mouse move event. When mouse up event happens, the window moved work is finished.

members.name: RequestResizeToServer
members.brief: Requests to display server for the window is resized by display server.
members.detailed: This function should be called in mouse down event callback function. After this function is called in mouse down event callback function, the window is resized with mouse move event. The direction is selected one of eight ways. When mouse up event happens, the window resized work is finished.

params.name: direction
params.description: it is indicated the window's side or edge for starting point.

members.name: EnableFloatingMode
members.brief: Enables the floating mode of window.
members.detailed: The floating mode is to support making partial size window easliy. It is useful to make popup style window and this window is always upper than the other normal window. In addition, it is easy to change between popup style and normal style window. A special display server(as a Tizen display server) supports this mode.

members.name: IsFloatingModeEnabled
members.brief: Returns whether the window is floating mode or not.
members.returns: True if the window is enabled floating mode, false otherwise.

members.name: IncludeInputRegion
members.brief: Includes input region.
members.detailed: This function inlcudes input regions. It can be used multiple times and supports multiple regions. It means input region will be extended. This input is related to mouse and touch event. If device has touch screen, this function is useful. Otherwise device does not have that, we can use it after connecting mouse to the device.
members.signature: void IncludeInputRegion(Window window, const Rect< int > &inputRegion)

params.name: inputRegion
params.description: The added region to accept input events.

members.name: ExcludeInputRegion
members.brief: Excludes input region.
members.detailed: This function excludes input regions. It can be used multiple times and supports multiple regions. It means input region will be reduced. Nofice, should be set input area by IncludeInputRegion() before this function is used. This input is related to mouse and touch event. If device has touch screen, this function is useful. Otherwise device does not have that, we can use it after connecting mouse to the device.
members.signature: void ExcludeInputRegion(Window window, const Rect< int > &inputRegion)

params.name: inputRegion
params.description: The subtracted region to except input events.

members.name: SetNeedsRotationCompletedAcknowledgement
members.brief: Sets the necessary for window rotation Acknowledgement. After this function called, SendRotationCompletedAcknowledgement() should be called to complete window rotation.
members.detailed: This function is supprot that application has the window rotation acknowledgement's control. It means display server waits when application's rotation work is finished. It is useful application has the other rendering engine which works asynchronous. For instance, GlView. It only works on Tizen device.

params.name: needAcknowledgement
params.description: the flag is true if window rotation acknowledge is sent.

members.name: SendRotationCompletedAcknowledgement
members.brief: send the Acknowledgement to complete window rotation. For this function, SetNeedsRotationCompletedAcknowledgement should be already called with true.

members.name: FeedTouchPoint
members.brief: Feed (Send) touch event to window.
members.signature: void FeedTouchPoint(Window window, const Dali::TouchPoint &point, int32_t timeStamp)

members.name: FeedWheelEvent
members.brief: Feed (Send) wheel event to window.

members.name: FeedKeyEvent
members.brief: Feed (Send) key event to window.

params.name: keyEvent
params.description: The key event holding the key information.

members.name: FeedHoverEvent
members.brief: Feed (Send) hover event to window.

params.name: point
params.description: The touch point that create a hover event

members.name: Maximize
members.brief: Maximizes window's size.
members.detailed: If this function is called with true, window will be resized with screen size. Otherwise window will be resized with previous size. It is for the window's MAX button in window's border. It is for client application. If window border is supported by display server, it is not necessary.

params.name: maximize
params.description: If window is maximized or unmaximized.

members.name: IsMaximized
members.brief: Returns whether the window is maximized or not.
members.returns: True if the window is maximized, false otherwise.

members.name: SetMaximumSize
members.brief: Sets window's maximum size.
members.detailed: It is to set the maximized size when window is maximized or the window's size is increased by RequestResizeToServer(). Although the size is set by this function, window's size can be increased over the limitation by SetPositionSize() or SetSize(). After setting, if Maximize() is called, window is resized with the setting size and move the center.

members.name: Minimize
members.brief: Minimizes window's size.
members.detailed: If this function is called with true, window will be iconified. Otherwise window will be activated. It is for the window's MIN button in window border. It is for client application. If window border is supported by display server, it is not necessary.

params.name: minimize
params.description: If window is minimized or unminimized(activated).

members.name: IsMinimized
members.brief: Returns whether the window is minimized or not.
members.returns: True if the window is minimized, false otherwise.

members.name: SetMimimumSize
members.brief: Sets window's minimum size.
members.detailed: It is to set the minimum size when window's size is decreased by RequestResizeToServer(). Although the size is set by this function, window's size can be decreased over the limitation by SetPositionSize() or SetSize().

members.name: MaximizeWithRestoreSize
members.brief: Sets the maximized state of a given window with specific size.
members.detailed: If this function is called with true, window will be resized with screen size. Otherwise window will be resized with restore size. It is for the window's MAX button in window's border. It is for client application. If window border is supported by display server, it is not necessary.
members.signature: void MaximizeWithRestoreSize(Window window, bool maximize, Dali::Window::WindowSize restoreSize)

params.name: maximize
params.description: If window is maximized or unmaximized.

params.name: restoreSize
params.description: This is the size used when unmaximized.

members.name: IsWindowRotating
members.brief: Query whether window is rotating or not.
members.returns: true if window is rotating, false otherwise.

members.name: GetLastKeyEvent
members.brief: Gets the last key event the window gets.
members.returns: The last key event the window gets.

members.name: GetLastTouchEvent
members.brief: Gets the last touch event the window gets.
notes: It returns the raw event the window gets. There is no hit-actor and local position information.
members.returns: The last touch event the window gets.

members.name: GetLastHoverEvent
members.brief: Gets the last hover event the window gets.
notes: It returns the raw event the window gets. There is no hit-actor and local position information.
members.returns: The last hover event the window gets.

members.name: GetLastPanGestureState
members.brief: Gets the last pan gesture state the window gets.
members.returns: The last pan gesture state the window gets.

members.name: PointerConstraintsLock
members.brief: Sets the pointer constraints lock.

members.name: PointerConstraintsUnlock
members.brief: Sets the pointer constraints unlock.

members.name: LockedPointerRegionSet
members.brief: Sets the locked pointer region.
members.signature: void LockedPointerRegionSet(Window window, int32_t x, int32_t y, int32_t width, int32_t height)

members.name: LockedPointerCursorPositionHintSet
members.brief: Sets the locked pointer cursor position hintset.
members.signature: void LockedPointerCursorPositionHintSet(Window window, int32_t x, int32_t y)

members.name: PointerWarp
members.brief: Sets the pointer warp. The pointer moves to the set coordinates.
members.signature: bool PointerWarp(Window window, int32_t x, int32_t y)

members.name: CursorVisibleSet
members.brief: Sets visibility on/off of cursor.

members.name: KeyboardGrab
members.brief: Requests grab key events according to the requested device subtype.

members.name: KeyboardUnGrab
members.brief: Requests ungrab key events.

members.name: SetFullScreen
members.brief: Sets full screen sized window. If full screen size is set for the window, window will be resized with full screen size. In addition, the full screen sized window's z-order is the highest.

params.name: fullscreen
params.description: true to set fullscreen, false to unset.

members.name: GetFullScreen
members.brief: Gets whether the full screen sized window or not.
members.returns: Returns true if the full screen sized window is.

members.name: SetFrontBufferRendering
members.brief: Enables or disables front buffer rendering.

params.name: enable
params.description: true to enable front buffer rendering, false to disable.

members.name: GetFrontBufferRendering
members.brief: Gets whether front buffer rendering is enabled.
members.returns: Whether front buffer rendering has been enabled or not.

members.name: SetModal
members.brief: Enables or disables the modal of window.

params.name: modal
params.description: true to enable the modal of window, false to disable.

members.name: IsModal
members.brief: Returns whether the window has a modal or not.
members.returns: True if the window is modal, false otherwise.

members.name: SetAlwaysOnTop
members.brief: Enables or disables the window always is on top.
members.detailed: This is valid between windows that have no notification level or a notification level of 'none'. If it has a notification level, this will not do anything.

params.name: alwaysOnTop
params.description: true to enable the window always is on top, false to disable.

members.name: IsAlwaysOnTop
members.brief: Returns whether the window always is on top.
members.returns: True if the window always is on top, false otherwise.

members.name: SetBottom
members.brief: Enables or disables the window's layer is changed to bottom.
members.detailed: If the enable flag is true, this window will be placed below other windows. Otherwise, if it's called with a false value, it will be located above other windows.

params.name: enable
params.description: true to change the window layer to the bottom.

members.name: IsBottom
members.brief: Returns whether the window layer is the bottom or not.
members.returns: True if the window layer is the bottom, false otherwise.

members.name: GetNativeBuffer
members.brief: Gets the native buffer of the window.
members.detailed: When users call this function, it wraps the actual type used by the underlying window system.
members.returns: The native buffer of the Window or an empty handle

members.name: RelativeMotionGrab
members.brief: Requests relative motion grab.
members.returns: True if the request was successful, false otherwise.

params.name: boundary
params.description: The Pointer edge boundary for grab.

members.name: RelativeMotionUnGrab
members.brief: Requests relative motion ungrab.
members.returns: True if the request was successful, false otherwise.

members.name: SetBlur
members.brief: Sets the window blur.

members.name: GetBlur
members.brief: Gets the window blur.

members.name: GetInsets
members.brief: Gets the window insets for all parts of the system UI.
members.returns: The window insets from all parts.

members.name: GetInsets
members.brief: Gets the combined window insets for the specified parts of the system UI.
members.returns: The combined window insets from the specified parts.

params.name: insetsFlags
params.description: A bitwise combination of WindowInsetsPartFlags values specifying which window insets parts to include.

members.name: SetScreen
members.detailed: Set the screen for this window This method sets the window to the specified screen. The window will be moved to the specified screen.

params.name: window
params.description: The window wants to change the screen.

params.name: screenName
params.description: The name of the screen for this window

members.name: GetScreen
members.detailed: Get the screen of window. It is for multiple screen environment.
members.returns: The name of the screen as a string.

members.name: SetForceRendering
members.brief: Request to rendering forcibly for this window.

params.name: frameCount
params.description: The number of frames to render forcibly.

members.name: InterceptKeyEventSignal
members.brief: The user would connect to this signal to intercept a KeyEvent at window.
members.detailed: Intercepts KeyEvents in the window before dispatching KeyEvents to the control. If a KeyEvent is consumed, no KeyEvent is delivered to the control.

members.name: MouseInOutEventSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when the mouse in or out event is received.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,Dali::MouseInOutEventevent);

members.name: InsetsChangedSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when window insets are changed by appearing or disappearing indicator, virtual keyboard, or clipboard.

members.name: MouseRelativeEventSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when the mouse relative event is received.
members.detailed: A callback of the following type may be connected: voidYourCallbackName(Windowwindow,Dali::MouseRelativeEventevent);

members.name: PointerConstraintsSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when pointer is locked/unlocked.

members.name: KeyEventMonitorSignal
members.brief: This signal is emitted when the key event monitor is received.

members.name: GetAvailableScreens
members.brief: Retrieves a list of all currently available screens.
members.detailed: This function queries the window system for all connected and active screens, providing essential information for each. It is primarily intended for use in multi-screen environments where an application might need to manage windows across different displays.
members.returns: A std::vector containing Dali::ScreenInformation objects for each available screen. If no screens are available, the returned vector will be empty.

members.name: SetGraphicsBackend
members.brief: Sets the graphics backend.
members.detailed: Generally, calling this is not required and can be set using the environment variable (DALI_GRAPHICS_BACKEND) instead. If called before the Application class is created, then the environment variable is ignored.
notes: This can only be called once and only before the graphics backend has been created (i.e. before the Application Class is started). If called again or after the graphics backend has started, then the call will not change anything.
Currently has no effect as the Graphics backend is chosen at compile time

compounds.name: Dali::KeyGrab
compounds.brief: Key grab functions.

members.name: KeyGrabMode
members.brief: Enumeration for key grab mode for platform-level APIs.

enumvalues.name: TOPMOST
enumvalues.brief: Grab a key only when on the top of the grabbing-window stack mode.

enumvalues.name: SHARED
enumvalues.brief: Grab a key together with the other client window(s) mode.

enumvalues.name: OVERRIDE_EXCLUSIVE
enumvalues.brief: Grab a key exclusively regardless of the grabbing-window's position on the window stack with the possibility of overriding the grab by the other client window mode.

enumvalues.name: EXCLUSIVE
enumvalues.brief: Grab a key exclusively regardless of the grabbing-window's position on the window stack mode.

members.name: GrabKeyTopmost
members.brief: Grabs the key specified by key for window only when window is the topmost window.
members.detailed: This function can be used for following example scenarios: Mobile - Using volume up/down as zoom up/down in camera apps.
members.signature: DALI_ADAPTOR_API bool GrabKeyTopmost(Window window, Dali::KEY daliKey)

params.name: daliKey
params.description: The key code to grab (defined in key.h)

members.name: UngrabKeyTopmost
members.brief: Ungrabs the key specified by key for window.
notes: If this function is called between key down and up events of a grabbed key, an application doesn't receive the key up event.
members.signature: DALI_ADAPTOR_API bool UngrabKeyTopmost(Window window, Dali::KEY daliKey)

params.name: daliKey
params.description: The key code to ungrab (defined in key.h)

members.name: GrabKey
members.brief: Grabs the key specified by key for window in grabMode.
members.detailed: @PLATFORM This function can be used for following example scenarios: TV - A user might want to change the volume or channel of the background TV contents while focusing on the foreground app. Mobile - When a user presses Home key, the homescreen appears regardless of current foreground app. Mobile - Using volume up/down as zoom up/down in camera apps. @PRIVLEVEL_PLATFORM @PRIVILEGE_KEYGRAB
members.signature: DALI_ADAPTOR_API bool GrabKey(Window window, Dali::KEY daliKey, KeyGrabMode grabMode)

params.name: daliKey
params.description: The key code to grab (defined in key.h)

params.name: grabMode
params.description: The grab mode for the key

members.name: UngrabKey
members.brief: Ungrabs the key specified by key for window.
members.detailed: @PLATFORM @PRIVLEVEL_PLATFORM @PRIVILEGE_KEYGRAB
notes: If this function is called between key down and up events of a grabbed key, an application doesn't receive the key up event.
members.signature: DALI_ADAPTOR_API bool UngrabKey(Window window, Dali::KEY daliKey)

params.name: daliKey
params.description: The key code to ungrab (defined in key.h)

members.name: GrabKeyList
members.brief: Grabs the list of keys specified by Dali::Vector of keys for window in Dali::Vector of grabModes.
members.detailed: @PLATFORM This function can be used for following example scenarios: TV - A user might want to change the volume or channel of the background TV contents while focusing on the foreground app. Mobile - When a user presses Home key, the homescreen appears regardless of current foreground app. Mobile - Using volume up/down as zoom up/down in camera apps. @PRIVLEVEL_PLATFORM @PRIVILEGE_KEYGRAB
members.signature: DALI_ADAPTOR_API bool GrabKeyList(Window window, const Dali::Vector< Dali::KEY > &daliKeyVector, const Dali::Vector< KeyGrabMode > &grabModeVector, Dali::Vector< bool > &returnVector)

params.name: daliKeyVector
params.description: The Dali::Vector of key codes to grab (defined in key.h)

params.name: grabModeVector
params.description: The Dali::Vector of grab modes for the keys

params.name: returnVector
params.description: The Dali::Vector of return boolean values for the results of multiple grab succeeds/fails

members.name: UngrabKeyList
members.brief: Ungrabs the list of keys specified by Dali::Vector of keys for window.
members.detailed: @PLATFORM @PRIVLEVEL_PLATFORM @PRIVILEGE_KEYGRAB
notes: If this function is called between key down and up events of a grabbed key, an application doesn't receive the key up event.
members.signature: DALI_ADAPTOR_API bool UngrabKeyList(Window window, const Dali::Vector< Dali::KEY > &daliKeyVector, Dali::Vector< bool > &returnVector)

params.name: daliKeyVector
params.description: The Dali::Vector of key codes to ungrab (defined in key.h)

params.name: returnVector
params.description: The Dali::Vector of return boolean values for the results of multiple ungrab succeeds/fails

compounds.name: Dali::SamplingMode
compounds.brief: Filtering options, used when resizing images to sample original pixels.
compounds.detailed: A SamplingMode controls how pixels in an input image are sampled and combined to generate each pixel of a destination image during a scaling. NoFilter and Box modes do not guarantee that the output pixel array exactly matches the rectangle specified by the desired dimensions and FittingMode, but all other filter modes do if the desired dimensions are <= the raw dimensions of the input image file.

members.name: Script
members.brief: Script is the writing system used by a language. Typically one script can be used to write different languages although one language could be written in different scrips.

enumvalues.name: COMMON
enumvalues.brief: Valid for all scripts. i.e white space or ' '.

enumvalues.name: C1_CONTROLS
enumvalues.brief: Controls of the C1 Controls and Latin-1 Supplement unicode block.

enumvalues.name: C1_PS
enumvalues.brief: Punctuation and symbols of the C1 Controls and Latin-1 Supplement unicode block.

enumvalues.name: C1_MATH
enumvalues.brief: Math symbols of the C1 Controls and Latin-1 Supplement unicode block.

enumvalues.name: SML_P
enumvalues.brief: Punctuation symbols of the Spacing Modifier Letters unicode block.

enumvalues.name: PHONETIC_U
enumvalues.brief: Uralic phonetic symbols of the Phonetic Extensions unicode block.

enumvalues.name: PHONETIC_SS
enumvalues.brief: Subscripts and superscripts of the Phonetic Extensions unicode block.

enumvalues.name: LETTER_LIKE
enumvalues.brief: Symbols of the Letterlike unicode block.

enumvalues.name: FRACTIONS_NF
enumvalues.brief: Numeric fraction symbols of the Number Forms unicode block.

enumvalues.name: NON_LATIN_LED
enumvalues.brief: Non latin symbols within the Latin Extended D unicode block.

enumvalues.name: HWFW_S
enumvalues.brief: Non latin symbols within the Halfwidth and fullwidth unicode block.

enumvalues.name: CYRILLIC
enumvalues.brief: The Cyrillic script. Used by Russian, Bulgarian, Ukrainian, Macedonian, ...

enumvalues.name: GREEK
enumvalues.brief: The Greek script. Used by Greek.

enumvalues.name: LATIN
enumvalues.brief: The latin script. Used by many western languages and others around the world.

enumvalues.name: ARABIC
enumvalues.brief: The arabic script. Used by Arab and Urdu among others.

enumvalues.name: HEBREW
enumvalues.brief: The Hebrew script. Used by the Hebrew, Yiddish, Ladino, and Judeo-Arabic.

enumvalues.name: ARMENIAN
enumvalues.brief: The Armenian script. Used by Armenian.

enumvalues.name: GEORGIAN
enumvalues.brief: The Georgian script. Used by Georgian.

enumvalues.name: CJK
enumvalues.brief: The CJK script. Used by Chinese, Japanese, Korean and Vietnamese(old writing system).

enumvalues.name: HANGUL
enumvalues.brief: The Hangul jamo script. Used by Korean.

enumvalues.name: HIRAGANA
enumvalues.brief: The Hiragana script. Used by the Japanese.

enumvalues.name: KATAKANA
enumvalues.brief: The Katakana script. Used by the Japanese.

enumvalues.name: BOPOMOFO
enumvalues.brief: The Bopomofo script. Also called Zhuyin fuhao or Zhuyin. A phonetic notation used for the transcription of spoken Chinese.

enumvalues.name: BENGALI
enumvalues.brief: The Bengali script. Used by Bangla, Assamese, Bishnupriya Manipuri, Daphla, Garo, Hallam, Khasi, Mizo, Munda, Naga, Rian, and Santali.

enumvalues.name: BURMESE
enumvalues.brief: The Burmese script. Used by the Burmese (Myanmar) language.

enumvalues.name: DEVANAGARI
enumvalues.brief: The devanagari script. Used by Hindi, Marathi, Sindhi, Nepali and Sanskrit.

enumvalues.name: GUJARATI
enumvalues.brief: The Gujarati script. Used by Gujarati.

enumvalues.name: GURMUKHI
enumvalues.brief: The Gurmukhi script. Used by Punjabi.

enumvalues.name: KANNADA
enumvalues.brief: The Kannada script. Used by Kannada and Tulu.

enumvalues.name: MALAYALAM
enumvalues.brief: The Malayalam script. Used by Malayalam.

enumvalues.name: ORIYA
enumvalues.brief: The Oriya script. Used by Oriya (Odia), Khondi, and Santali.

enumvalues.name: SINHALA
enumvalues.brief: The Sinhala script. Used by Sinhala and Pali.

enumvalues.name: TAMIL
enumvalues.brief: The Tamil script. Used by Tamil, Badaga, and Saurashtra.

enumvalues.name: TELUGU
enumvalues.brief: The Telugu script. Used by Telugu, Gondi, and Lambadi.

enumvalues.name: LAO
enumvalues.brief: The Lao script. Used by the Lao language.

enumvalues.name: THAI
enumvalues.brief: The Thai script. Used by the Thai language.

enumvalues.name: KHMER
enumvalues.brief: The Khmer script. Used by the Khmer language.

enumvalues.name: JAVANESE
enumvalues.brief: The Javanese script. Used by the Javanese language.

enumvalues.name: SUNDANESE
enumvalues.brief: The Sundanese script. Used by the Sundanese language.

enumvalues.name: GEEZ
enumvalues.brief: The Ge'ez script. Used by the Amharic, Tigrinya and other languages in Ethiopia and Eritrea.

enumvalues.name: OL_CHIKI
enumvalues.brief: The Ol Chiki script. Used by the Santali.

enumvalues.name: BAYBAYIN
enumvalues.brief: The Baybayin script. Used by the Tagalog, Bikol languages, Ilocano, Pangasinan, Visayan and other languages in Philippines.

enumvalues.name: MEITEI
enumvalues.brief: The Meitei script used for the Meitei language in Manipur, India.

enumvalues.name: EMOJI
enumvalues.brief: The Emoji which map to standardized Unicode characters.

enumvalues.name: EMOJI_TEXT
enumvalues.brief: The Emoji request a text presentation for an emoji character.

enumvalues.name: EMOJI_COLOR
enumvalues.brief: The Emoji request a color-emoji presentation for an emoji character.

enumvalues.name: SYMBOLS_NSLCL
enumvalues.brief: THe Negative Squared Latin Capital Letter.

members.name: IsEmojiComponent
members.brief: Whether the character is emoji component. Emoji Properties and Data Files: https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr51/#Emoji_Properties_and_Data_Files.
notes: True for characters used in emoji sequences that normally do not appear on emoji keyboards as separate choices, such as keycap base characters or Regional_Indicator characters.
members.returns: true if the character is emoji component.

members.name: IsEmojiModifier
members.brief: Whether the character is emoji modifier. Emoji Properties and Data Files: https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr51/#Emoji_Properties_and_Data_Files.
members.returns: true if the character is emoji modifier.

members.name: IsCombiningEnclosingKeycap
members.brief: Whether the character is COMBINING ENCLOSING KEYCAP. Combining Enclosing Keycap: https://emojipedia.org/combining-enclosing-keycap/.
notes: The Codepoint of COMBINING ENCLOSING KEYCAP is {U+20E3}
members.returns: true if the character is COMBINING ENCLOSING KEYCAP.

members.name: IsRegionalIndicator
members.brief: Whether the character is Regional Indicator. Regional Indicator Symbol Letter A: https://emojipedia.org/regional-indicator-symbol-letter-a/ Regional Indicator Symbol Letter Z: https://emojipedia.org/regional-indicator-symbol-letter-z/.
notes: The Codepoints of Regional Indicator Symbol Letters [A-Z] are [U+1F1E6 - U+1F1FF]
members.returns: true if the character is Regional Indicator.

members.name: IsTextPresentationSelector
members.brief: Whether the character is text presentation selector (VARIATION SELECTOR-15). Text Presentation Selector: https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr51/#def_text_presentation_selector.
notes: The character U+FE0E VARIATION SELECTOR-15 (VS15), used to request a text presentation for an emoji character. Also known as text variation selector.
members.returns: true if the character is text presentation selector (VARIATION SELECTOR-15).

members.name: IsEmojiPresentationSelector
members.brief: Whether the character is emoji variation selector (VARIATION SELECTOR-16). Emoji Presentation Selector: https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr51/#def_emoji_presentation_selector.
notes: The character U+FE0F VARIATION SELECTOR-16 (VS16), used to request an emoji presentation for an emoji character. Also known as emoji variation selector.
members.returns: true if the character is emoji presentation selector (VARIATION SELECTOR-16).

members.name: IsTagSpec
members.brief: Whether the character is tag spec. Emoji Tag Sequence (ETS): https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr51/#def_emoji_tag_sequence.
notes: The tag_spec consists of all characters from U+E0020 TAG SPACE to U+E007E TAG TILDE. Each tag_spec defines a particular visual variant to be applied to the tag_base character(s). Though tag_spec includes the values U+E0041 TAG LATIN CAPITAL LETTER A .. U+E005A TAG LATIN CAPITAL LETTER Z, they are not used currently and are reserved for future extensions.
members.returns: true if the character is tag spec.

members.name: IsTagEnd
members.brief: Whether the character is tag end. Emoji Tag Sequence (ETS): https://www.unicode.org/reports/tr51/#def_emoji_tag_sequence.
notes: The tag_end consists of the character U+E007F CANCEL TAG, and must be used to terminate the tag sequence.
members.returns: true if the character is tag end.

members.name: IsEmojiItem
members.brief: Whether the character is emoji-component | emoji-modifier | COMBINING-ENCLOSING-KEYCAP | regional-indicator | TextPresentationSelector | EmojiPresentationSelector | TagSpec | TagEnd.
members.returns: true if the character is emoji-component | emoji-modifier | COMBINING-ENCLOSING-KEYCAP | regional-indicator | TextPresentationSelector | EmojiPresentationSelector | TagSpec | TagEnd.

members.name: IsMiscellaneousSymbolsAndArrowsEmoji
members.brief: Whether the character is Miscellaneous Symbols and Arrows emoji. Miscellaneous Symbols and Arrows emoji: https://unicode-table.com/en/blocks/miscellaneous-symbols-and-arrows/.
notes: The Range is 2B00—2BFF and the quantity of characters is 256.
members.returns: true if the character is Miscellaneous Symbols and Arrows emoji.

members.name: IsDingbatsEmoji
members.brief: Whether the character is Dingbats emoji. Dingbats emoji: https://unicode-table.com/en/blocks/dingbats/.
notes: The Range is 2700—27BF and the quantity of characters is 192.
members.returns: true if the character is Dingbats emoji.

members.name: IsASCIIDigits
members.brief: Whether the character is ASCII digits. ASCII digits: https://unicode-table.com/en/blocks/basic-latin/.
notes: The Range is 0030—0039 and the quantity of characters is 10.
members.returns: true if the character is ASCII digits.

members.name: IsASCIIPS
members.brief: Whether the character is ASCII PS. ASCII PS: https://unicode-table.com/en/blocks/basic-latin/.
notes: The Range is 0020-007E
members.returns: true if the character is ASCII PS.

members.name: IsEmojiVariationSequences
members.brief: Whether the character is emoji-variation-sequences. Emoji Variation Sequences for UTS #51: https://www.unicode.org/Public/14.0.0/ucd/emoji/emoji-variation-sequences.txt.
members.returns: true if the character is emoji-variation-sequences.

members.name: IsNegativeSquaredLatinCapitalLetter
members.brief: Whether the character is Negative Squared Latin Capital Letter. Read more at White on black squared Latin letters: https://unicode-table.com/en/blocks/enclosed-alphanumeric-supplement/.
members.returns: true if the character is Negative Squared Latin Capital Letter.

members.name: IsSymbolScript
members.brief: Whether the script is symbol.
notes: Check whether the script is in [SYMBOLS1, SYMBOLS2, SYMBOLS3, SYMBOLS4, SYMBOLS5]
members.returns: true if the script is symbol.

members.name: IsEmojiScript
members.brief: Whether the script is EMOJI.
members.returns: true if the script is EMOJI.

members.name: IsEmojiTextScript
members.brief: Whether the script is EMOJI_TEXT.
members.returns: true if the script is EMOJI_TEXT.

members.name: IsEmojiColorScript
members.brief: Whether the script is EMOJI_COLOR.
members.returns: true if the script is EMOJI_COLOR.

members.name: IsOneOfEmojiScripts
members.brief: Whether the script is EMOJI | EMOJI_COLOR| EMOJI_TEXT.
members.returns: true if the script is EMOJI | EMOJI_COLOR| EMOJI_TEXT.

members.name: IsSymbolOrEmojiScript
members.brief: Whether the script is symbol or emoji.
notes: At least one of IsSymbol and IsEmoji is true.
members.returns: true if the script is symbol or emoji.

members.name: IsSymbolOrEmojiOrTextScript
members.brief: Whether the script is SYMBOL | EMOJI | TEXT.
notes: At least one of IsSymbol and IsEmoji and IsText is true.
members.returns: true if the script is SYMBOL | EMOJI | EMOJI_COLOR| EMOJI_TEXT.

members.name: FontClientPreInitialize
members.brief: This is used to improve application launch performance.

members.name: FontClientPreCache
members.brief: This is used to pre-cache FontConfig in order to improve the runtime performance of the application.
members.signature: void FontClientPreCache(const FontFamilyList &fallbackFamilyList, const FontFamilyList &extraFamilyList, const FontFamily &localeFamily, bool useThread, bool syncCreation)

params.name: fallbackFamilyList
params.description: A list of fallback font families to be pre-cached.

params.name: extraFamilyList
params.description: A list of additional font families to be pre-cached.

params.name: localeFamily
params.description: A locale font family to be pre-cached.

params.name: useThread
params.description: True if the font client should create thread and perform pre-caching, false otherwise.

params.name: syncCreation
params.description: True if thread creation guarantees syncronization with the main thread, false async creation.

members.name: FontClientFontPreLoad
members.brief: This is used to pre-load FreeType font face in order to improve the runtime performance of the application.
members.detailed: The fonts in the memoryFontPathList read the font file and cache the buffer in memory during pre-load. This enables the use of FT_New_Memory_Face during runtime and provides a performance boost. It requires memory equivalent to the size of each font file.
notes: The fonts in the fontPathList perform FT_New_Face during pre-loading, which can provide some performace benefits.
members.signature: void FontClientFontPreLoad(const FontPathList &fontPathList, const FontPathList &memoryFontPathList, bool useThread, bool syncCreation)

params.name: fontPathList
params.description: A list of font paths to be pre-loaded.

params.name: memoryFontPathList
params.description: A list of memory font paths to be pre-loaded.

params.name: useThread
params.description: True if the font client should create thread and perform font pre-loading, false otherwise.

params.name: syncCreation
params.description: True if thread creation guarantees syncronization with the main thread, false async creation.

members.name: FontClientJoinFontThreads
members.brief: Joins font threads, waiting for their execution to complete.

members.name: EnsureLocale
members.brief: Ensure the locale of the font client.
notes: If there is no locale information, update it using setlocale().

members.name: GetLocale
members.brief: Gets the current language.
notes: Returns the language code. (e.g., "en")

members.name: GetLocaleFull
members.brief: Gets the current locale identifier.
notes: Returns the locale identifier. (e.g., "en_US")

members.name: SetLocale
members.brief: Sets the current locale.
notes: Update language and locale identifier.

members.name: EnableDesignCompatibility
members.brief: Enable font specific design compatibility mode globally.
members.detailed: When enabled, FontMetrics will adjust ascender/descender values at construction to ensure design compatibility rules.
notes: This is a one-way switch. Once enabled, it cannot be disabled again during the lifetime of the process. This function must be called before any text is rendered.

members.name: DesignCompatibilityEnabled
members.brief: Whether DesignCompatibility is enabled or not.

compounds.name: Dali::VirtualKeyboard
compounds.brief: This namespace is provided for application developers to be able to show and hide the on-screen keyboard.
compounds.detailed: Key events are sent to the actor in focus. Focus is set through the actor Public API.

members.name: TextDirection
members.brief: The direction of text.

members.name: ReturnKeyType
members.brief: The meaning of the return key.

members.name: Show
members.brief: Show the virtual keyboard.

members.name: Hide
members.brief: Hide the virtual keyboard.

members.name: IsVisible
members.brief: Returns whether the virtual keyboard is visible or not.

members.name: ApplySettings
members.brief: Set one or more of the Input Method Settings.

params.name: settingsMap
params.description: Map of Settings to be applied.

members.name: SetReturnKeyType
members.brief: Set the specific return key into the virtual keyboard.

params.name: type
params.description: the kind of return key types.

members.name: GetReturnKeyType
members.brief: Retrieve the current return key type.

members.name: EnablePrediction
members.brief: Enable/disable prediction (predictive text).
members.detailed: By default prediction text is enabled.

params.name: enable
params.description: true or false to enable or disable Prediction can not be changed while the keyboard is visible. It must be set in advance of showing keyboard.

members.name: IsPredictionEnabled
members.brief: Returns whether prediction is enabled in the virtual keyboard.
members.returns: true if predictive text enabled, false otherwise.

members.name: GetSizeAndPosition
members.brief: Provides size and position of keyboard.
members.detailed: Position is relative to whether keyboard is visible or not. If keyboard is not visible then position will be off the screen. If keyboard is not being shown when this method is called the keyboard is partially setup (IMFContext) to get the values then taken down. So ideally GetSizeAndPosition() should be called after Show().
members.returns: rect which is keyboard panel x, y, width, height

members.name: RotateTo
members.brief: Rotates the keyboard orientation to the given angle.
members.detailed: A value of 0 indicates the portrait orientation. Other valid values are 90, 180, 270.

members.name: GetTextDirection
members.brief: Returns text direction of the keyboard's current input language.
